summary: Crypto wallet stealer with hardcoded C2 and worm self-propagation
details: Package ships three malicious modules. scanner.js collects host identity (os.hostname(), `whoami` via execSync) and POSTs/HTTPS-GETs to a hardcoded GitHub Pages C2 at https://ddjidd564.github.io, with curl-based fallbacks. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem enumeration (fs.existsSync paths) consistent with browser/wallet data harvesting and posts results to attacker-controlled endpoints across multiple POST sites. worm-injector.js (named for its function) imports child_process across multiple locations and performs hardcoded-destination POSTs — the self-propagation stage that injects the payload into other packages on the host. The package name 'mnemonic-safety-check' is a lure aimed at crypto users; the shipped behavior is wallet/seed data theft and worm-style spreading rather than any safety check. Installing or loading this package causes immediate harvesting of host identity and wallet-related filesystem state and exfiltration to attacker infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
